candidates.name: Steve Farley
candidates.bio: Steve Farley (born December 24, 1962) is an American politician, artist, and graphic designer who previously served as a State Senator from Arizona, including serving in the capacity of assistant minority leader. He also served previously in the Arizona House of Representatives, serving as assistant minority leader there as well. He is a member of the Democratic Party.

candidates.name: Anthony Sabatini
candidates.bio: Anthony Frank Sabatini (born October 20, 1988) is an American far-right politician, attorney, and Florida Army National Guard officer serving as a Lake County commissioner since 2024. A member of the Republican Party, he previously served in the Florida House of Representatives from 2018 to 2022. Once a strong supporter of President Donald Trump, Sabatini has been a critic of Donald Trump since his endorsement of Daniel Webster over himself in 2024.
key_messages: Sabatini has called for a ban on all immigration to the United States except from Italy.
Sabatini rejects the scientific consensus on climate change and has called for the execution of climate change activists.

Sabatini has opposed gun control and supported unrestricted carry legislation allowing concealed firearms without permits, describing permit requirements as forcing people to 'beg government permission' for a 'fundamental God-given right.'
Sabatini called to defund the Federal Bureau of Investigation, stating that GOP candidates and officials should pledge to 'defund these lawless agencies.'

campaign_themes.heading: immigration
campaign_themes.text: Sabatini has called for a ban on all immigration to the United States except from Italy.

campaign_themes.text: Sabatini rejects the scientific consensus on climate change and has called for the execution of climate change activists.

campaign_themes.text: Sabatini has opposed gun control and supported unrestricted carry legislation allowing concealed firearms without permits, describing permit requirements as forcing people to 'beg government permission' for a 'fundamental God-given right.'

campaign_themes.heading: criminal_justice
campaign_themes.text: Sabatini called to defund the Federal Bureau of Investigation, stating that GOP candidates and officials should pledge to 'defund these lawless agencies.'

platform_excerpts.excerpt: Sabatini has called to ban all immigration to the United States except from Italy.
Sabatini rejects the scientific consensus on climate change and has called for the execution of climate change activists.

Sabatini has opposed gun control in the United States and has supported unrestricted carry legislation to allow Floridians to carry concealed firearms without first acquiring a permit. Sabatini described permit requirements as a requirement for persons 'to beg government permission and pay money for the exercise of their fundamental God-given right.'

In 2022, Sabatini called to defund the Federal Bureau of Investigation, saying: 'I think every single candidate and elected official in the GOP right now needs to go on record pledging to defund these lawless agencies.'

top_stances.summary: Ban all immigration except from Italy; opposes unrestricted immigration to the United States.
top_stances.track_record_note: No voting record available; stance based on campaign platform statements.

top_stances.summary: Rejects scientific consensus on climate change; advocates extreme measures against climate activists.
top_stances.track_record_note: No voting record available; stance based on campaign platform statements.

top_stances.summary: Opposes gun control; supports unrestricted concealed carry without permits as a fundamental right.
top_stances.track_record_note: No voting record available; stance based on campaign platform statements.

top_stances.summary: Call to defund the FBI; opposes federal law enforcement agencies as lawless.
top_stances.track_record_note: No voting record available; stance based on campaign platform statements.

candidates.name: Daniel Webster
candidates.bio: Daniel Webster (January 18, 1782 – October 24, 1852) was an American lawyer, statesman, and diplomat who represented New Hampshire and Massachusetts in the U.S. Congress and served as the 14th and 19th U.S. secretary of state under presidents William Henry Harrison, John Tyler, and Millard Fillmore. Webster was one of the most prominent American lawyers of the 19th century, arguing over 200 cases before the United States Supreme Court in his career.
